## Session Notes — June 1, 2026 (sitemap automation + Bing + workflow discoveries)

**Sitemap is now auto-generated by a GitHub Action — no more hand-editing.**
- New files: `.github/workflows/generate-sitemap.yml` + `.github/scripts/generate-sitemap.js`. Live and confirmed working — a `sitemap-bot` "Auto-update sitemap [skip ci]" commit appeared in the history on the first run.
- The script scans every `index.html` → clean URL, special-cases `login.html` → `/login/`, excludes `admin/`, `versions/`, `.github/`, and ignores partials (`_header.html`, `_footer.html`, `404.html`, `email-logo-snippet.html`). Produces 27 URLs — including `/tools/` and all blog posts, which the old hand-maintained sitemap was missing.
- To adjust later: edit `EXTRA_PAGES` (standalone non-index pages like login) and `EXCLUDE_DIRS` (folders to hide) near the top of the `.js`.
- **Deliberately NO `<lastmod>`, `<priority>`, or `<changefreq>`.** Google ignores priority/changefreq. `lastmod` was dropped on purpose so the sitemap only rewrites on page ADD / REMOVE / RENAME, never on a content edit — this is what keeps it from colliding with `version-bump.yml` (which fires on content edits). The push step has a pull-rebase-retry safety net for the rare simultaneous-commit case. **`version-bump.yml` was left untouched.**

**Decision: GitHub Action, NOT a Cloudflare build step.** Verified the Cloudflare Pages `notavello` project has Build command / Build output / Root dir all **BLANK** — it serves the repo as-is, no build step. Adding one would have risked the live deploy. The Action commits `sitemap.xml` back to the repo and Cloudflare serves it like any other file; Cloudflare config is never touched. (Cloudflare shows the connected repo as `skychael/chatscript`; production branch `main`, automatic deployments on.)

**Auto-versioning (roadmap priority #5) is ALREADY DONE.** Discovered `version-bump.yml` already exists and runs: on edits to root + the 7 exporter `index.html` files it bumps the `xxx-N.N` version string + timestamp and commits with a `[version-bump]` message (guarded against re-triggering on its own commits via path filter + message check). No further work needed here.

**Bing Webmaster Tools:** site is verified; the URL Submission API was noticed. **Decision: SKIP it for now** — Bing's share is small, the site is still changing, and the manual "Submit URLs" button covers it. Revisit only if blog posting becomes frequent (could then be a small Worker ping on deploy).

**Git habit note:** bot commits (`sitemap-bot`, `version-bump`) land on GitHub's side, so the local clone falls "behind." In GitHub Desktop, click **Fetch origin → Pull** before starting new edits to avoid the "pull before push" rejection. This is normal, not an error.

**Misc:** `/pages/blog/ubuntu-4-digit-pin-login/` now exists in the repo (the sitemap picked it up). New post `google-drive-vs-onedrive-file-transfer-developers` was added this session.

---

### Two small edits to the existing sections higher up in HANDOFF.md

- **Known Remaining Issues** → delete the line *"`sitemap.xml` may need updating to include `/tools/` and `/pages/blog/`"* — now handled automatically.
- **Active Development Priorities** → mark **#5 Auto-versioning** as **[DONE]** (`version-bump.yml` is live). Sitemap upkeep is now automated as well.
